Compare the 'Pull Model' (calculating feed on read) vs 'Push Model' (calculating feed on write). Which is better for users with few followers?
How do you handle 'Celebrity Status' (users with millions of followers)? Fanning out to 100M users simultaneously will crash the system.
Consider a hybrid approach: push posts to most users, but let fans of celebrities pull those specific posts when they open their feed.
What's your storage strategy? Posts are immutable and read-heavy. NoSQL databases like Cassandra are popular for this.
